contract.privacy_boundary: Interop requires hashes, specs, receipts, and redacted artifact refs; raw prompts, raw private assets, and private evidence are not required.

domains.purpose: Seeded visual systems for repeatable variation, critique, and replay.
Wireframes, shaded primitives, old-computer display language, and measurement overlays.
Dither, pixel sorting, halftone, glitch, CRT, chromatic, contour, and export filters.
Web Audio and DFT-backed sound generation, sonification, and audio-reactive visuals.
Shot/frame transforms, poster systems, film texture, and media export receipts.
Display typography, generative glyph studies, and font-lab style experiments.
Flow fields, harmonographs, n-dimensional geometry, particles, and measurable constants.
TouchDesigner-compatible recipes and modular effect graphs for app, TUI, and IDE hosts.
Scene specs, action identities, receipt chains, replay, and MATCH/DRIFT/UNVERIFIABLE checks.

techniques.claim: Ordered threshold texture for low-bit previews and export stress tests.
Luminance-ranked row fragments that make raster transforms inspectable.
Dot-gain and press-screen texture for poster and print previews.
Additive wave and spectral tools can become sound layers with receipts.
Kilon and Conso presentation can seed future generated glyph and specimen tools without committing purchased font files.
Effects can be represented as modular recipe nodes for visual-programming hosts.
Seeded particle and flow-field sketches expose sensitivity and perturbation maps.
Screen clusters and light bins preview a future clustered-forward renderer.

techniques.interop: CLI/MCP renderer profile and fallback contract
techniques.claim: Hosts can select WebGPU, WebGL2, canvas, or static artifact rendering with explicit fallback, privacy, and verification gates.
Histogram, dither, splat, cluster, and audio meters expose measurable state for creative and scientific artifacts.

techniques.interop: CLI/MCP kernel packet and reusable ES module
techniques.claim: Ordered dithering, pixel sorting, harmonograph paths, and clustered light bins are available as deterministic kernels with receipt hashes.

rendering_capabilities.boundary: Renderer capability selection is separate from verification. A selected GPU path is an execution choice; claims still require receipts, benchmarks, and MATCH/DRIFT/UNVERIFIABLE checks.

measurement_layers.boundary: Measurement layers expose evidence signals. Crucible still owns verdicts, and raw private assets are not required for interop.

live_modules.role: Dependency-free browser creative layer for file, web, and app surfaces.
Scene spec, preset, action identity, receipt hash, and replay protocol.
Deterministic ordered-dither, pixel-sort, plotter, and clustered-light kernels for shared engine hosts.
N-dimensional geometry, projection, raster, and WebGL organ.
Sound synthesis and DFT organ for sonification and audio-reactive work.
Perception, feature, pyramid, distribution, and fusion organ.
Subject, regulator, reconcile, embed, certificate, and flagship loop organ.

sensor_measurement_layers.why: Dithering, poster, and glitch work need measurable tone structure instead of only aesthetic description.
Dithering is a core creative primitive and needs measurable structure for ordered, void-and-cluster, and blue-noise inspired paths.
Splat scenes should be inspectable as captured data, not just pretty radiance-field images.
Clustered or Forward+ rendering needs measurement gates for visibility, performance, and overlay readability.
Sound-related creative work should expose spectral state for models, artists, and replay checks.

revival_candidates.why: Bring n-dimensional geometry, depth, projection, raster, and WebGL back into Studio scenes.
Restore sound generation and spectral analysis as first-class creative layers.
Use feature extraction and fusion to let models inspect creative artifacts instead of only describe them.
Keep creative work tied to reconcile, certificate, and regulator primitives.
Recover raster effects, dithering, quantization, and image transform code.
Connect visual constants to sound and audio proofs.
Use flow fields for generative motion, sensitivity maps, and scientific overlays.
Restore plotter-friendly oscillator drawings and pendulum studies.
Emit browser-native audio graphs from engine recipes.
Recover media presentation and capture utilities for the public demo.
Reuse tested sound rendering code in the Telos Studio without duplicating it.

roadmap.focus: Restore dormant dithering, pixel sorting, media, sound, flow, harmonograph, and raster organs into runnable adapters.
Represent effects, media, audio, and verification as node-graph recipes usable from IDE, CLI, TUI, MCP, and app hosts.
Add typography, glyph, poster, film, audio-reactive, and scientific-art generators with export receipts.
Every creative action emits scene specs, hashes, receipt chains, replay handles, and MATCH/DRIFT/UNVERIFIABLE results.

presentation.source_lead_boundary: Design critique communities are treated as operator-provided inspiration leads; no community artwork is copied into the repository.

research_receipts.mission: Assist mathematicians, physicists, shader artists, and educators with formula-visible, replayable, receipt-backed creative and scientific demos.
research_receipts.boundary: Video metadata is a source lead. Scientific, benchmark, model-release, and implementation claims require stronger source receipts and Crucible assessments before promotion to MATCH.
